steps.title: Find the required verb
steps.content: The clause this phrase ______ roughly to "it's an old story" needs a main finite verb. The subject is this phrase, which is singular and third person, so the completed clause needs a verb form that can agree with that subject.

steps.title: Use a finite singular verb
steps.content: Choice A, to translate, is an infinitive, so it cannot serve by itself as the main verb of the clause. Choice B, translating, is a participle or gerund and would need an auxiliary verb to form a predicate. Choice C, having translated, is also non-finite and does not create a complete main clause after the subject. Choice D, translates, is a third-person singular present-tense verb, so it agrees with this phrase and completes the sentence. The answer is D.
